rationale: Abstract Y is slightly stronger overall. Both abstracts define a specific, testable gap and report numerical comparisons with IQRs and p values. Y has a clearer stated objective, cleaner JICE-style structure, more consistent units, and a better-calibrated conclusion because it labels the findings exploratory and limits the implication to procedural precision during CNA. X is close, but its conclusion overreaches by implying improved outcomes despite no outcome data being presented.
loser_weakness: Abstract X most lacked certainty calibration: the final sentence claims support for improving outcomes, which is stronger than the retrospective 10-patient mapping comparison can justify.
